subject: feat(theme): burger menu stays open during theme picker interactions
body:
Closes the one actionable gap found in the glow-props comparison: our
burger menu closed after every item click, including theme picker clicks,
forcing users to reopen the menu to try a second theme. glow-props
deliberately omits the `data-close` attribute on theme controls so the
menu stays open — users can rapid-preview multiple themes in a single
menu session. Adopting the same UX.

Changes:

1. HamburgerMenu.handleItem() now takes the full item object and checks
   item.keepOpen. Two paths:

     - keepOpen: true → run the action immediately, skip close(), skip
       the 150 ms setTimeout. Used for theme picker items and the
       dark/light mode toggle so users can rapid-preview multiple themes
       or switch modes then pick a theme without reopening the menu.

     - default → close the menu first, then run the action after 150 ms
       (matches the CSS fade animation so state changes from the action
       don't cause visual glitches while the menu is fading out). Used
       for Quick Guide, User Guide, Save as PDF, Install App, Update Now.

   Shared error handling extracted into a runAction(action) helper so
   both paths route errors through debugAdd('render', 'error', ...).

   Call site updated from handleItem(item.action) to handleItem(item).
   handleItem is internal to HamburgerMenu; no other callers.

   JSDoc updated to document the new keepOpen item field.

2. Header.jsx menuItems memo now sets keepOpen: true on:

     - The dark/light mode toggle item. Users can toggle modes and then
       pick a theme for the new mode in a single menu session. The theme
       list below the toggle swaps to the new mode's themes when the
       toggle action dispatches.

     - All 4 theme picker items for the current mode. Clicking Nord →
       theme applies, menu stays open, Nord is now marked active with
       checkmark. Clicking Emerald → theme applies, menu stays open,
       Emerald is now marked active. All 4 themes previewable in 4 clicks
       from a single menu open.

   Other menu items (Quick Guide, User Guide, Save as PDF, Install App,
   Update Now) keep their existing close-then-act behavior — only theme
   controls got keepOpen.

Why this is safe:

- Menu items are keyed by item.label in HamburgerMenu's render loop, and
  theme names are stable across re-renders. When activeThemeId changes
  after a picker click, React reconciles the buttons in place — the
  clicked button's DOM element is preserved, focus stays on it, only
  icon/highlight/ariaLabel props update.

- The ariaLabel change ("Use Nord theme (Cool blue-gray)" → "Use Nord
  theme (Cool blue-gray), currently active") provides natural
  screen-reader feedback confirming the theme was applied.

- click-outside, Escape, and the backdrop click handler all still work
  — keepOpen only suppresses the per-item auto-close. Users can always
  dismiss the menu.

- The 150 ms setTimeout path still applies to non-keepOpen items so
  close-animation-vs-action ordering is preserved for those.

Things from glow-props deliberately NOT adopted (documented in docs/HISTORY.md):

- 35-theme catalog. Our curated 4+4 is the right tradeoff for a utility
  app per the reference doc's own advice.
- Random theme on load. Wrong for an analytics dashboard.
- Auto-classify from color-scheme. Only helps with full catalogs; we
  already validate against DaisyUI's classification.
- Regex-based multi-file rewrite. Fragile to reformatting.
- Manual generator invocation. We prebuild for dev/build/build:lib.

Build and tests:
- npm test: 21 passing, 0 failing, 6 suites, ~145 ms
- npm run build: passes, 84 modules. CSS 160.06 KB (unchanged — no
  CSS change this pass). JS 555.75 → 556.03 KB (+0.28 KB for the
  refactored handleItem logic + keepOpen: true literals).
- Generator idempotent — all 4 downstream files report "unchanged"
- Smoke test via vite preview + curl verified: keepOpen identifier
  survived minification into the JS bundle; all 8 theme names,
  descriptions, aria-labels, CSS theme selectors, and flash
  prevention hex values still present; all 11 critical custom class
  families still emitted.

Documentation updated:
- docs/HISTORY.md — full entry with rationale for every not-adopted item
- docs/SESSION_NOTES.md — sixth pass summary at the top
- docs/TESTING_GUIDE.md — 4 new test scenarios: rapid-preview behavior,
  mode toggle keepOpen, focus preservation after theme click, non-theme
  items still close the menu
- docs/USER_GUIDE.md — updated Theme section to mention rapid-preview

Tags: theme, daisyui, ui, a11y, refactor
Complexity: 2
Urgency: 1
Impact: user-facing
Risk: low
Debt: paid
Epic: daisyui-migration
Semver: minor
